title: Prim, Kruskal, Wilson, Bias, and Distribution Claims · Program Brief: Generator Selection
summary: Algorithms are judged from one attractive screenshot, “random” is called uniform, and difficulty is inferred from generation time. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the generator selection increment in comparative seeded generation laboratory. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select a generator from player, performance, reproducibility, topology, and accessibility goals with explicit non-guarantees.
Measure corridor length, turns, dead ends, path stretch, branching, and distributions across many recorded seeds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-generator-selection — Select a generator from player, performance, reproducibility, topology, and accessibility goals with explicit non-guarantees.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-generation-alternatives-bias-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-generation-alternatives-bias-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_generation_alternatives_bias_4(seeds: int = 64, valid_trees: int = 64, algorithms: int = 4, measured_metrics: int = 5, uniformity_claim_bounded: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if seeds < 16 or valid_trees < 0:
        return False, valid_trees, "sample-budget"
    if valid_trees != seeds:
        return False, valid_trees, "invalid-generated-tree"
    if algorithms < 4 or measured_metrics < 4:
        return False, algorithms, "comparison-coverage"
    if not uniformity_claim_bounded:
        return False, measured_metrics, "distribution-overclaim"
    assert valid_trees == seeds
    return True, valid_trees, "bounded-generator-comparison"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-generator-selection-kuha0u
# Competency: maze-generator-selection.
# Case 1945: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def evidence_maze_generator_selection_kuha0u(seeds: int = 64, valid_trees: int = 64, algorithms: int = 4, measured_metrics: int = 5, uniformity_claim_bounded: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_kuha0u = "kuha0u-u0ahuk-1945"
    assert evidence_variant_kuha0u
    evidence_axes_kuha0u = (
        "gridkuha0u",
        "topology1945",
        "searchu0ahuk",
        "eventkuha0u1945",
        "access1945kuha0u",
        "recoveryu0ahuk1945",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_kuha0u)) == 6
    if seeds < 16 or valid_trees < 0:
        return False, valid_trees, "sample-budget"
    if valid_trees != seeds:
        return False, valid_trees, "invalid-generated-tree"
    if algorithms < 4 or measured_metrics < 4:
        return False, algorithms, "comparison-coverage"
    if not uniformity_claim_bounded:
        return False, measured_metrics, "distribution-overclaim"
    assert valid_trees == seeds
    return True, valid_trees, "bounded-generator-comparison"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Select a generator from player, performance, reproducibility, topology, and accessibility goals with explicit non-guarantees.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Generator Selection model · Theory Generator Selection, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Generator Selection precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Generator Selection model · Theory Generator Selection, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 8285 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare multiple generators across seeded topology and bias evidence without unsupported distribution claims; all graph claims need an independent witness; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Select a generator from player, performance, reproducibility, topology, and accessibility goals with explicit non-guarantees.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Generator Selection model · Theory Generator Selection, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: The fastest generator is automatically the best game design.
